segments.s683-g153: سپاه is the subject meaning 'the host/army'. اندرآمد is a past-tense verbal phrase (classical/poetic) meaning 'entered' or 'came in' (here attached as one word); ز هر پهلوی is a prepositional phrase 'from every flank/side' modifying the verb. Word order is slightly inverted in epic style: subject (سپاه) + verb (اندرآمد) + source (ز هر پهلوی).
segments.s683-g154: همی‌سوختند is a classical/poetic imperfective marker همی- plus the past/plural verb سوختند 'were burning' (progressive/continuous aspect). آتش is the theme 'fire(s)' and appears after the verb by inversion for emphasis; از هر سوی is a source phrase 'from every side/direction' that specifies where the fires blazed. The clause reads naturally as 'fires were burning from every direction.'
segments.s683-g105: ستوران is a collective noun 'the animals / livestock' followed by the quantifier/particle همه 'all'; adjectives خسته و کوفته follow the noun and are linked by و 'and'. کوفته here is adjectival/predicative meaning 'beaten/worn out' (not the food sense). This phrase functions as the subject/description of the following locative/state phrase.
segments.s683-g106: زراه is the prepositional/ablative phrase 'from the way / from the road' (ز = 'from') with دراز 'long' postmodifying راه. اندر is the locative 'in/within', and آشوفته (poetic/archaic spelling of آشفته) is a past-participial/adjectival form meaning 'disturbed / in disarray'. Together this gives the circumstantial clause describing state caused by travel.
segments.s683-g107: بدین is the compound 'بِ + دین' (here functioning as the demonstrative 'to/into this' in poetry) meaning 'to this'; خانه is its object 'house'; سوکواران is the plural agent 'the archers'; به رنج is an adverbial phrase 'with/through hardship' (به + noun). Word order is poetic: direction ('to this house') then agent then manner.
segments.s683-g108: فرود آمدستیم is a compound verbal expression: فرود (descent/alighting) plus آمدستیم, the past 1st‑person‑plural 'we came / we descended' (archaic/poetic past form). با introduces the accompanying phrase 'with', and یار is 'companion' (here followed by the numeral پنج in inverted poetic order: 'with five companions'). The whole clause states the action and company.
segments.s683-g109: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'; چوپیدا here means 'when ... is found / when it appears' (چو + پیدا/compound). شود is the present/subjunctive or future auxiliary 'becomes / is', and چاک is used as a noun 'a split / rent'; روز سپید is a noun phrase 'the bright/white day' (سپید as adjective after روز). The clause is conditional/temporal setting for the main verb.
segments.s683-g110: کنم is the 1st‑person singular present/subjunctive 'I will/do (make)'; دل is the direct object 'heart'; زکار is a prepositional/explanatory phrase 'from/because of the deeds/affairs' (ز = از 'from/because of' + کار); جهان modifies کار 'of the world'; ناامید is the resulting predicate 'hopeless / despairing'. The structure means 'I will make my heart hopeless because of the world's affairs.'
segments.s683-g111: بیاییم is a hortative/optative first‑person plural 'let us come / let us go' (subjunctive used as an exhortation). با تو is the accompaniment 'with you'. به راه دراز is a directional phrase 'onto/to the long road', with دراز postmodifying راه. Word order is idiomatically poetic but follows Persian patterns (verb first for exhortation).
segments.s683-g112: به نزدیک is a prepositional phrase 'toward the vicinity / near to'; به here functions as 'towards' and نزدیک is 'near'. بهرام is the proper name 'Bahram', and گردن‌فراز is a descriptive epithet 'proud / high‑necked' placed after the name. The whole phrase gives the destination/goal of the proposed movement.
segments.s683-g25: بخوردند is a past 3rd‑person plural verb (they ate/they swallowed) placed at the start for emphasis. با شتاب is an adverbial prepositional phrase ('with haste/quickly'). چیزی که بود is a relative phrase 'that which was/whatever there was' functioning as the object of بخوردند.
segments.s683-g26: پس آنگه are sequential adverbs ('then/afterwards') reinforcing the temporal sequence. به marks the indirect object ('to'), here followed by زمزم used as a vocative/name. بگفتند is the past plural 'they said'; زود is an adverb (here functioning as the content of their utterance or as an imperative: 'quickly').
segments.s683-g27: چنین گفت (lit. 'thus said') is a formulaic way to introduce direct speech; چنین is an adverb 'thus/so' preceding the verb. پس is a transitional 'then'. با + سکوبا marks the person addressed ('to/with the cupbearer'); سکوبا here is an obscure/rare term likely meaning the cupbearer (sense chosen accordingly). که introduces the content clause; می in this position is the noun 'wine' (not the verbal prefix), starting the clause about having wine.
segments.s683-g28: نداری here functions syntactically as the 2nd‑person negative ('you do not have'); in classical/poetic Persian the form looks identical to the noun senses in the lexicon, so the exact listed senses do not match (marked -1). تو‌ای is the vocative 'O you' (poetic). پیرفرخنده is an epithet 'auspicious/cheerful old man' addressing the addressee. پی at the end is likely a clipped or emphatic particle/part of the epithet and is unclear in the provided senses, so marked -1.
segments.s683-g29: بدو here means 'to him / unto him' (i.e., 'to that person he said'); گفت is the past 'said' though the provided lexicon senses don't include the verbal sense (marked -1). ما is the first‑person plural pronoun 'we'. می‌زخرما is a compound noun 'date‑wine' (the beverage they intend to prepare). کنیم is the 1st‑person plural present/subjunctive/optative ('we will/may do' or 'we shall make') expressing intention.
segments.s683-g30: به تموز marks time ('in Tammuz' — here the month), وهنگام is a connective phrase 'and at the time (of)', گرما is 'heat'/'the hot season', and کنیم repeats the verbal form 'we will/may do' (same verb as previous segment) indicating when they will prepare the date‑wine.
segments.s683-g31: کنون هست is a present existential 'now there is' (کنون = now, هست = is). لختی is an adverbial noun 'for a little while/briefly'. چو روشن introduces a simile ('as/like bright'), and گلاب is the subject/noun 'rosewater' described as bright.
segments.s683-g32: به سرخی is a phrase 'in redness' or 'of a redness'; چو is the comparative 'like/as'. بیجاده literally 'without road' (here metaphorical — 'remote' or 'off the beaten path'). در آفتاب 'in the sun' completes the simile: the rosewater's redness compared to sunlight on a roadless place. Word order follows Persian simile patterns (چو + adjective/noun).
segments.s683-g41: همان چون = a temporal connective meaning 'just as' or 'as soon as' (همان here gives immediacy). بخواب اندر آمد is a poetically compressed verbal phrase: بخواب ('into sleep') + اندر آمد ('entered/came in'), i.e. 'sleep came upon/entered him'. سرش is head + the enclitic ش = 'his', so 'upon his head / to his head'. Word order is typical older Persian: temporal phrase → verb → affected noun with possessive enclitic.
segments.s683-g42: سکوبای مهتر is a title + apposition: سکوبای likely a (rare) proper name or title meaning something like 'Sekubā' or 'cupbearer', followed by مهتر 'the chief/elder'. بیامد is past tense 'came'. برش here is an archaic compressed form meaning 'بر او' = 'to him / upon him' (not the literal modern noun 'cut'); it functions as the directional/objective phrase 'came to him'.
segments.s683-g43: که begins a subordinate/explanatory clause 'for/that'. از راه گردی = 'from the road's dust' (از = from, راه = road, گردی = dust); برآمد is past 'arose/issued forth'. سیاه here qualifies the dust/cloud as 'dark' or 'black'—poetic coloring of the dust cloud. Word order: background conjunction → source phrase → verb → modifier.
segments.s683-g44: دران = 'in that/within it' (referring to the dust/cloud just mentioned). گرد here repeats the idea of 'dust' (هَوا/گرد = dust), تیره = 'dark/ominous', فراوان = 'plentiful/abundant', سپاه = 'host/army'. The phrase thus specifies that within that dark dust there was a numerous army; adjective order is post-nominal as typical in Persian poetry.
segments.s683-g45: چنین گفت خسرو is the formula 'thus said Khosrow' (چنین = thus, گفت = said). که introduces direct speech or a clause explaining his words. بد روزگار is a classical idiom meaning 'evil times' or 'unlucky fate' (بد modifies روزگار). Note: گفت is a past-tense verb in modern Persian ('he said'), though the provided senses list seems noun-based; here it functions as 'said'.
segments.s683-g46: This clause continues his complaint: که دشمن بدین گونه شد خواستا ر. بدین گونه = 'in this manner / thus' (here split in manuscript into ب + دین + گونه; treat as the fused prepositional/ adverbial 'بدین گونه'). شد = 'became' (resultative). خواستا ر is an older form meaning 'desirous/intent' or 'eager' (خواستا ~ خواستار). The final ر is the enclitic/ra-like relic marking relation; the whole reads roughly 'that the enemy has become thus, desirous (to do so)'.
segments.s683-g47: نه ... و نه = 'neither ... nor'. مردم به کارست = 'people fit for the task' or 'people useful for the work' (مردم = people; به = for/to; کارست is an archaic adjective/noun meaning 'of use / capable'). نه بارگی: بارگی here refers to provisions/loads or the capacity to bear burdens (i.e. supplies or support); the phrase denies the presence of either capable people or resources.
segments.s683-g48: فراز آمد is a verbal idiom meaning 'rose up' or 'came forth' (فراز 'on high/above' used idiomatically with آمد). آن روز بیچارگی = 'that day of misery' (آن = that; روز = day; بیچارگی = misery/misfortune). Together: 'that day of helplessness/misery came upon them.'
segments.s683-g73: بکرد is a 3rd-person past tense verb ('he did'). آن زمان is an adverbial time phrase ('at that time'); note the adjective آن is placed before زمان as in classical Persian. هرچ is an indefinite relative ('whatever'); بندوی is the object/agent referred to ('the bondman/servant'). The clause order is literary: verb (بکرد) introduces the sentence, followed by the time phrase and the indefinite relative clause that supplies what was done.
segments.s683-g74: وزانجایگه ('وز آن جایگه' contracted) functions adverbially → 'thereupon/from that place'. گشت is a past verb meaning 'went/turned' (literal 'became/was turned' in older Persian). با + باد + جفت is a compact image: با باد جفت = 'paired/joined with the wind' (جفت used as 'pair'/'matched with'); overall this short clause describes departing together with the wind.
segments.s683-g75: چو introduces a temporal clause ('when'). خسرو is the proper name/title 'the king'. برفت is the verbal past 'departed' (here the lexical entry given is nonstandard; it functions as 'he went away'). از marks origin/ separation ('from'); بر in this phrase functions idiomatically as 'the (royal) presence' or 'court' (classical usage), and چاره جوی is a noun+participle compound meaning 'seeking a remedy' (چاره = remedy, جوی = seeker/searching). The whole unit means 'when the king left the presence seeking a remedy.'
segments.s683-g76: جهاندیده is an epithet ('the worldly‑wise/experienced one') used as subject. سوی is a prepositional word 'toward'; سقف is the noun 'roof/ceiling'; کرد + روی is a light-verb construction where کرد (to do/make) combines with روی ('face') to mean 'turned his face' or 'looked toward'. The verb is simple past formed by کرد here.
segments.s683-g77: که introduces a subordinate/explanatory clause ('that'/'for that'). اکنون is temporal ('now'). شما را uses the object/topic marker را after شما to mark the person affected by the upcoming necessity. ب + دین here corresponds to classical بدین ('by this'/'in this way'); the input lexemes are split but combine to mean 'by this'. بر ز کوه is a slightly inverted/poetic order meaning 'from the mountain' (ز = 'from', کوه = 'mountain'); the whole phrase means 'that now you, by this, from the mountain...'.
segments.s683-g78: بباید شدن is a periphrastic modal expression ('must become' / 'it is necessary that ... become') where بباید expresses obligation and شدن is the infinitive/auxiliary 'to become'. ناپدید is a predicative adjective/participle meaning 'vanished/unseen'. از گروه is a prepositional phrase 'from the company/troop' marking whose sight one leaves. Together: 'you must become unseen from the group.'
segments.s683-g79: خود is emphatic/reflexive ('himself'). اندر is the classical preposition 'in/within'. پرستشگه is 'place of worship/shrine'. آمد is the simple past 'came/entered'. چو here is a comparative/simile 'as' or 'when' and گرد in this phrase is the noun 'dust' or figuratively 'around/about'; the clause connects the arrival to his going about the shrine ('he entered the shrine and, like dust, went around' / 'he circled about').
segments.s683-g80: بزودی is an adverb 'quickly/soon'. در here is the noun 'door/gate' (not the preposition), so در آهنین = 'the iron door'. سخت کرد is a light-verb construction where سخت ('hard/firm') with کرد ('made') yields 'made fast/firm' or 'secured' (i.e., he firmly shut/fastened the iron door). The clause means 'he quickly made the iron door fast.'
segments.s683-g33: هم آنگه is an adverbial phrase (poetic/stylistic) meaning 'then' or 'at that moment' placed before the verb to set the temporal scene. بیاورد is a third-person singular past/action-form (classical usage: 'he brought' / 'he produced') and takes جامی نبید as its object; جامی is جام + ـی (indefinite/partitive) 'a cup', and نبید (poetic word) names the content 'wine' and follows the container noun in this terse poetic order.
segments.s683-g34: که introduces a relative/explanatory clause 'such that / so that'. شد is the past of شدن 'became'. رنگ خورشید is a possessive/content phrase 'the color of the sun' (noun + noun), ز (poetic از) is the preposition 'from/of', and the final و ناپدید is a terse poetic contraction meaning 'from it vanished' — ناپدید 'vanished/hidden'. The line thus states result: the wine's redness made the sun's color disappear beside it.
segments.s683-g35: بخورد is the past action-verb (from خوردن) here meaning 'he ate/drank/took'; in classical Persian خورد can cover both 'eat' and 'drink'. آن زمان is a temporal phrase 'at that time'. خسرو is the subject. از governs the following phrase 'from the cup' and می‌سه جام is the object introduced by از (poetic compound: 'from the wine-cup'). Word order is somewhat compressed in poetic diction: verb + time + subject + prepositional phrase.
segments.s683-g36: This segment lists what was taken/consumed: می‌و نان کشکین is a coordinated/juxtaposed noun phrase ('wine and kashkīn bread'). کشکین is an adjective or descriptive noun 'kashkīn' (here 'made with/like kashk' or a kind of food) modifying نان. The relative clause که دارد بنام ('which has/is called by name') follows to name or label that provision; دارد is present/persistent 'has' or 'is endowed with', and بنام (بـ نام) means 'called / by the name'.
segments.s683-g37: چو here means 'when'. مغزش is مغز + ـش 'his brain' (possessive clitic). شد is 'became'. از باده gives the cause 'from the wine'. سرخ گرم are two adjectives stacked after the cause meaning 'red, hot/warm' describing the brain; in classical Persian multiple adjectives may follow the noun introduced by شد.
segments.s683-g38: هم آنگه again is an adverbial 'and then' / 'at once'. بخفت is past 'he slept' (classical poetic past). از بر is a fixed older/pre-classical locative phrasing ('on/upon' with the poetic preposition بر following از), and ریگ نرم is the noun + adjective 'soft sand' (ریگ 'sand' + نرم 'soft'); the whole phrase means 'he laid down / fell asleep upon the soft sand.' The subject is carried over from the previous clause (he = Khosrow).
segments.s683-g39: نهاد here is the verbal past 'placed / put' (poetic past form). از بر ران بندوی is the prepositional phrase 'upon the thigh of the bondman' (ران بندوی = 'the bondman's thigh'); classical poetry often uses the order 'از بر X' for 'on X'. سر is the direct object 'head' (what he placed). The sequence shows verb + locative phrase + object in compressed poetic word order.
segments.s683-g40: روانش is روان + ـش 'his soul/spirit' (possessive). پر از is an idiom 'full of' taking the following noun (درد 'pain/sorrow'). The conjunction و links the next adjectival phrase خسته جگر, where جگر (literally 'liver') is used metaphorically as 'heart/seat of feeling' and خسته 'weary' qualifies it; together they mean 'and (his) heart was weary'. The construction therefore reports his inner state after sleeping.
segments.s683-g9: 'چنین' is an adverb meaning 'thus' and sets the manner of the action. 'تا' here functions as 'up to' / 'as far as' (a spatial limit). 'بِنزد' is an older/prepositional form meaning 'near/to the presence of' and governs the following noun. 'رباطی' denotes the caravanserai (a stopping‑place), and 'رسید' is the past tense verb 'he reached/arrived' placed at the end as the clause's predicate (typical SOV order in classical Persian).
segments.s683-g10: 'سر تیغ' is a compound meaning 'the tip/point of the blade' (literally 'head of the sword'). 'دیوار او' is a genitive construction 'the wall of it/his' (the pronoun او refers back to the place/person previously mentioned). 'ناپدید' is a predicate adjective 'vanished / not visible' — there is no copula in the past, so it functions as the clause's verb-like predicate. The whole segment says the blade‑points on the wall were not seen by/for him.
segments.s683-g11: 'کجا' here functions as a locative/discourse marker meaning 'there/where (at that spot)'. 'خواندندیش' is an archaic/periphrastic form meaning 'they called it / they named it' (خواندند + enclitic -یش = 'it'). 'یزدان سرای' is a proper name composed of 'یزدان' (God) + 'سرای' (house), i.e., 'the House of God' — the text uses the compound as the place's name.
segments.s683-g12: 'پرستشگهی' is the noun 'place of worship / shrine'. 'بود' is the past copula 'was'. 'و' is the coordinating conjunction 'and', and 'فرخنده جای' is an adjectival phrase 'auspicious/fortunate place' (adjective before noun in poetry). The clause states the place's function and its blessed character.
segments.s683-g13: 'نشستنگه' is 'seat/place for sitting' (the location where people sit). 'سوکواران' is the plural 'archers'. 'بدی' in this line functions as a proper name (Badi) and identifies whose archers they are (classical texts often omit an ezāfe, so 'سوکوارانِ بدی' = 'Badi's archers').
segments.s683-g14: 'بدو' is the dative/clitic phrase 'to him / toward him'. 'در' normally means 'in' but here helps locate the persons present; the sequence 'سکوبا و مطران بدی' lists two persons present: 'Sokuba' (a personal name) and 'مطران بدی' = 'the metropolitan (bishop) Badi' — again 'بدی' is a name. The verb 'came/was present' is implied rather than expressed overtly.
segments.s683-g15: 'چنین' ('thus') modifies the utterance. 'گفت' is the reporting verb 'said' (here used for speech), placed before the speaker 'خسرو' in inversion common in poetry, and 'به یزدان‌پرست' marks the indirect object 'to the pious man / the God‑worshipper'.
segments.s683-g16: This is an indirect interrogative clause. 'که' introduces the content of the question ('what / that which'). 'از خوردنی' = 'of the edibles / among the foods'. 'چیست' is the copula interrogative 'what is (it)'. The final phrase 'کاید بدست' contains an archaic/poetic verbal form — here functioning idiomatically to mean 'is to be had / can be obtained' (so the whole asks what food can be obtained). 'بدست' can literally mean 'by/into the hand' but in this idiom indicates obtainment.
segments.s683-g81: بپوشید is a past (3rd‑person) verb meaning 'he put on / he wore' with the archaic prefix بـ that appears in epic Persian; پس is an adverb 'then'; جامه زرنگار is the direct object 'a garment woven/ornamented with gold' (زرنگار is a compound adjective). The clause order is verb + adverb + object, a common classical order where the subject is omitted because it is understood from the narrative.
segments.s683-g82: به سر is a prepositional phrase 'onto (his) head' (به = 'to/onto', سر = 'head'); برنهاد is a past transitive verb 'placed on / set upon'; افسر شهریار is a noun phrase 'the kingly helmet' (افسر = helmet, شهریار qualifies it). Word order here is adverbial phrase (به سر) + verb (برنهاد) + object (افسر شهریار).
segments.s683-g83: بران بام برشد literally 'upon that roof he rose/ascended': بران = 'upon that', بام = 'roof', برشد is the past 'he mounted/rose up'. The phrase نه بر آرزوی introduces a negated motive ('not out of desire/longing'), so the speaker is saying he ascended the roof not for longing but for another reason (which the following clause clarifies).
segments.s683-g84: سپه دید = 'he saw the army/host' (سپه = army, دید = saw). گرد اندورن چارسوی is an adverbial cluster: گرد = 'around', اندورن = 'within/inside', چارسوی = 'on the four sides/everywhere' — together 'all around within on all four sides.' This clause states the perceptual result of his having climbed the roof.
segments.s683-g85: همی‌بود is an imperfective/past continuous 'it continued / it was so'; تا introduces the temporal clause 'until'; لشکر رزمساز is the noun phrase 'the war‑prepared/ fighting host' (لشکر = army, رزمساز here describes it as war‑making or battle‑ready). The clause sets up the time span that ends with the following verb.
segments.s683-g86: رسیدند is a past plural verb 'they arrived/reached' agreeing with لشکر; نزدیک is an adverb/preposition 'near'; آن دژ فراز is the noun phrase 'that high fortress/citadel' (آن = that, دژ = fortress, فراز = on high/top/high). Word order is verb + adverbial + demonstrative + noun phrase describing the place approached.
segments.s683-g87: ابرپای خاست = 'the cloud‑rim/edge rose' (ابرپای = cloud‑horizon/edge, خاست = arose). آنگه 'then' links this event to what follows; از بام زود is an adverbial phrase 'quickly/from the roof' (از = from, بام = roof, زود = soon/quickly). The clause sets the scene and timing for the action in the next segment.
segments.s683-g88: تن خویشتن را is an emphatic definite object 'his own body/person' (تن = body, خویشتن = himself, را = object marker); به لشکر نمود is 'he showed/revealed to the army' (به = to, لشکر = army, نمود = showed/appeared). The object precedes the verb with the particle را, the verb نمود is past tense 'showed/appeared'.
segments.s683-g137: بیاساید is an adverbial/verbal-form meaning 'without resting; incessantly' (an archaic/poetic presentive). امروز و فردا here functions idiomatically as 'day after day' (repeated time), and پگاه ('dawn, early morning') specifies the time; the whole phrase is an adverbial modifier that qualifies the following verb (همی‌راند).
segments.s683-g138: همی‌راند is a classical progressive/perfective form with the prefix همی- (continuous aspect) + راند (third-person sg.), meaning 'he kept riding/was riding'. اندر میان سپاه is a prepositional phrase 'in/among the midst of the host' (اندر + میان = 'in the middle of'), which serves as the verb's complement.
segments.s683-g139: چنین گفت is a fixed formula 'thus said'; the verb precedes the subject (classical word order). بهرام is the subject and با مهتران ('with the chiefs') indicates the audience addressed — با here functions as 'to/with' in speech to denote those present.
segments.s683-g140: که introduces reported content ('that'). کاریست is the copular construction (کار + -است) meaning 'it is a matter/affair'; این is the demonstrative 'this' (subject of the predicate). هم ... هم is the correlative pair marking 'both ... and', so سبک و گران are coordinated adjectives ('light' and 'heavy') describing the affair — a deliberate oxymoron.
segments.s683-g141: چو here means 'if' (introducing a conditional clause). بر خسرو is the prepositional phrase 'upon/against Khosrow' and این کار is the object 'this affair'. گیریم is a first-person-plural optative/subjunctive ('if we take/impose'), so the clause reads 'if we impose this matter on Khosrow'. تنگ is a postpositive adjective/adverb meaning 'tight/pressing' describing the pressure applied.
segments.s683-g142: مگر here functions as a modal particle indicating a possibility/expectation ('perhaps'/'unless' in older usage). تیز گردد is a potential/future-minded clause ('tiز = swift/eager' + گردد = 'becomes'), and بیاید به جنگ is another verb phrase 'may come to battle' (بیاید — potential/optative; به جنگ = 'to the fight'). Together they express the possible result of the condition.
segments.s683-g143: بتنها is an adverb 'alone/only' placed before the noun phrase. تن او 'his body/person' is the topical noun, and یکی لشکرست is a copular phrase ('is one army/one host') where لشکرست = لشکر + است ('is a host'): word order places the predicate after the subject, typical of epic diction.
segments.s683-g144: Three coordinated epithets follow (joined by و): جهانگیر 'world-conquering' (an epithet/title), بیدار 'awake/alert' (vigilant), and کنداورست a compound epithet (کندآور-ست) used figuratively — literally 'one who digs/uncovers' but applied here as a laudatory quality (energetic, probing, or forceful). These adjectives all modify the preceding predicate 'one host'.
segments.s683-g1: چوبهرام is the subject (a proper name here); رفت is the simple past third-person singular 'went/entered.' اندر is the preposition 'into/within' placed before its object, and ایوان شاه is a genitive phrase 'the hall of the king' (noun + possessor). Word order is Subject — Verb — Prepositional phrase, with the possessor شاه following the head ایوان.
segments.s683-g2: گزین کرد is a two-word past-tense verbal complex (گزین + کرد) meaning 'chose' (گزین as root 'choose' with کرد as the past auxiliary). زان = 'from that' (demonstrative + preposition), لشکر = 'army/host' as the source from which he selected, and کینه‌خواه is a compound meaning 'those who desire revenge' (کینه 'grudge/hatred' + خواه 'desirer/wanter'). The whole segment functions as the direct object of گزین کرد: 'he chose from that army the vengeance-seekers.'
segments.s683-g3: This is a nominal phrase listing types and a number: زره‌دار 'armored (men)' and شمشیرزن 'sword-wielders' (شمشیر + زن, where زن here is the agentive 'one who strikes'), followed by the numeral سی‌هزار 'thirty thousand' giving the quantity. Persian often strings descriptive nouns and then the numeral.
segments.s683-g4: بدان here functions as 'to that end / to those [men]' (a deictic/goal expression), followed by تا introducing purpose 'so that/in order that.' شوند is subjunctive/plural of شدن 'become/go' ('they may become/come to be'), and از پس شهریار means 'after/behind the king' (از پس = 'after/behind', شهریار = 'the king'). Together: 'so that they might take position behind the king.'
segments.s683-g5: چنین 'thus/so' introduces the statement; لشکری is a noun 'army/troops'; نامبردار is an adjective meaning 'renowned/aforementioned'; and گرد here is a poetic/archaic word meaning a 'company/detachment' (used as a second noun in apposition). The phrase names the force that is about to be entrusted.
segments.s683-g6: ببهرام is 'to Bahram' (preposition + proper name indicating the recipient), پور is an attributive 'son/of the lineage', سیاوش is the personal name, and سپرد is the simple past 'entrusted/handed over.' The verb سپرد has an implicit subject (the previous agent) and its object is the army named in the prior segment.
segments.s683-g7: وزان is a conjunctive/deictic 'and from that / from that time' linking to what preceded. روی ... گرفت is an idiomatic verbal phrase 'to turn one's face toward / to take direction to' (روی 'face/direction' + گرفت 'took'), so خسرو بیابان گرفت = 'Khosrow turned to/took to the desert' (گرفت is simple past).
segments.s683-g8: همی is an emphatic/continuous adverb here ('continually/always'); از بد means 'because of the badness/ill will' (از + بد, with از = 'because of' in this clause); دشمنان 'enemies' is the agent causing harm; جان گرفت is a verbal phrase where جان 'life' is the patient and گرفت 'was taken/seized' indicates that his life was endangered or seized — together meaning 'his life was in danger/was harmed.' The overall sense: 'continually, because of the enemies' wickedness his life was imperiled.'
segments.s683-g97: چنین گفت is a fixed narrative formula meaning “thus said” (چنین = 'thus/so' before the verb; گفت = 3rd-singular past 'said'). کای is the archaic vocative particle 'O' addressing the addressees. رزمسازان نو is a vocative noun phrase: رزمسازان 'war-makers/warriors' with the postpositive adjective نو 'new' (adjective follows noun in Persian), together addressed by کای.
segments.s683-g98: کرا is an archaic interrogative 'whom' (object of the verb). خوانم is first‑person singular present/subjunctive form meaning 'I call/summon' (here used as 'shall I call' / 'whom shall I summon'). اندر شما 'among you' is a locative/prepositional phrase (اندر = 'among/in', شما = 'you' plural/polite). پیش رو functions as a compound meaning 'to the front/foremost' (پیش = 'before/in front', رو here marking the forward / leading position), modifying the object of خوانم.
segments.s683-g99: که introduces the content or reported clause 'that / for'. پیغام دارم is a simple present construction 'I have a message' (پیغام = 'message'; دارم = 'I have'). ز شاه جهان is a poetic ablative/agent phrase 'from the king of the world' (ز = 'from', شاه جهان = 'king of the world' or 'sovereign').
segments.s683-g100: بگویم is first‑person singular present/subjunctive/optative 'that I should say / let me say / I will tell' used to introduce the forthcoming report. شنیده is a past participle 'heard' (here functioning as 'what I have heard' / the report I heard). به پیش مهان is a locative phrase 'before the guests' (به پیش = 'in the presence of', مهان = 'guests'); the whole phrase modifies بگویم (where the speaker will relay the heard message in front of the guests).
segments.s683-g101: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. پور سیاووش is the noun phrase 'the son of Siavash' (پور used as 'son of' or 'son' in names). دیدش is past tense 'saw him' with the enclitic object pronoun -ش 'him' attached to the verb (subject is omitted and recovered from context). ببام is an interjection/exclamation (an archaic vocative/exclamatory particle) following the verb and marking the outcry.
segments.s683-g102: منم is emphatic 'I am' or 'I too' (the clitic -م in this older form combines pronoun and copula). پیش رو here forms a nominal/epithet 'the one in front' or 'the vanguard' (پیش = 'in front', رو functioning in the compound to indicate forward/leading position). گفت introduces direct speech 'said', and بهرام نام is an appositive order meaning 'Bahram is the name' i.e. 'my name is Bahram' (نام as predicate following the proper name).
segments.s683-g103: بدو گفت is an inverted classical order meaning 'he said to him' (بدو = 'to him/unto it', گفت = 'said'). گوید is a present-tense verb 'says' used in reported speech to introduce the royal utterance; جهاندار شاه is a title 'the world‑holder king' or 'sovereign king' (noun phrase subject of گوید). The sequence marks reported speech within the narrative.
segments.s683-g104: که here introduces the content of the quotation 'that'. من سخت پیچانم is a first‑person present clause 'I am greatly/harshly twisted/afflicted' — پیچانم is 1st‑person present from a classical/poetic root (here meaning 'am distressed/wearied'); سخت is an intensive adverb 'severely/sorely'. از رنج راه is an ablative phrase 'by/from the hardship(s) of the road' (از = 'from/by', رنج = 'hardship/suffering', راه = 'road/journey'), giving the cause of the speaker's distress.
segments.s683-g121: زرخشنده خورشید is a noun phrase with the adjective-like compound زرخشنده (lit. ‘gold-shining’) preceding خورشید (the head noun) — typical Persian modifier-before-noun order here. تا introduces the subordinate clause that will be negated by نباشد: تا تیره خاک = 'until/that (the) earth be dark' (تیره qualifies خاک).
segments.s683-g122: نباشد is a negative present/subjunctive/jussive form meaning roughly 'may it not be' or 'let it not be' (an optative/negative clause head). مگر introduces the exception 'except/unless'. رای یزدان پاک is a genitive (the counsel/plan of the pure God) that supplies the sole allowable exception to the negative clause.
segments.s683-g123: چو is a temporal conjunction here meaning 'when'. سالار is the subject 'the commander' and بشنید is the past-tense verb 'heard'. زو is the contracted poetic form of از + او ('from him'), followed by داستان 'the tale' (object). Word order is typical poetic Persian: conjunction + subject + verb + source + object.
segments.s683-g124: به گفتار او is a dative/agent-like phrase 'by/with his words' or 'to his speech' (به functions here as 'by/in the respect of'). گشت is the past of گشتن 'became/turned', and همداستان is a predicate adjective 'like-minded/agreed'. The sequence means 'he became like-minded with his speech' (i.e. agreed with what was said).
segments.s683-g125: دگر here qualifies 'هرکه' as 'other/any other' (contrastive). هرکه بشنید is a relative/conditional clause 'whoever heard', and گفتار اوی is 'his speech/words' (اوی is the enclitic pronoun functioning as possessive here). The clause sets up the people affected.
segments.s683-g126: پر از درد شد دل is a slightly inverted predicate: 'the heart became full of pain' (پر از درد = 'full of pain', شد = 'became', دل = 'heart'). ز کردار اوی is a causal/agentive prepositional phrase 'because of his deeds' (ز = poetic 'from/because of', کردار = 'deeds', اوی = 'his').
segments.s683-g127: فرود آمد is a compound verbal phrase meaning 'came down/descended' (فرود as the nominal/verb element + آمد). آن شب denotes the time 'that night' and بدانجا the destination 'to that place'; سپاه ('army') appears as the subject placed after the verb and adverbials — a common poetic order.
segments.s683-g128: همی‌داشتی is an archaic/imperfect form built on داشتن with the prefix همی (continuous/imperfective) and here means 'was keeping/kept' (past continuous sense). رای خسرو is the object in genitive order 'the counsel/plan of Khosrow' and نگاه functions as a noun meaning 'view/attention' (so the whole phrase = 'kept Khosrow's counsel in view/kept watch over Khosrow's plan').
segments.s683-g17: سکوبا is the subject placed before the verb (a name or title). بدو is the indirect object/clitic phrase 'to him' (دو = 'to + him') placed between subject and verb. گفت is the past-tense verb 'said'. کای is an archaic vocative 'O' addressing someone directly, and نامدار is an epithet 'renowned' that follows the vocative. The whole segment is an S–[IO]–V plus a vocative phrase.
segments.s683-g18: فطیرست is a predicative adjective with an enclitic/copular sense (‘is natural/simple’) — the -ست ending functions like the archaic/poetic form of است. با تره جویبار is a prepositional phrase 'with (با) herbs (تره) of the stream (جویبار)', the whole phrase modifying or specifying the kind of simple fare named by فطیرست.
segments.s683-g19: گرای دون is a noun phrase 'an ignoble/base inclination' (گرای = inclination, دون = low/base). که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/which'; شاید is a modal adverb 'perhaps'. بدین سان is the fixed phrase 'in this way' (بدین = 'by/with this', سان = 'manner'), and خورش is the noun 'food/dish' that is being described — word order is typical poetic inversion where manner precedes or frames the noun.
segments.s683-g20: مبادت is an optative/negative wish 'may it not be' (a conventional poetic formula). جز از = 'except from' (جز = except, از = from). نوشه این پرورش is a genitival phrase 'a draught/sip (نوشه) of this (این) nourishment/upbringing (پرورش)', functioning as the exception after جز از. The clause expresses a wish that the food not be of that sort except insofar as it is accompanied by this nourishing draught.
segments.s683-g21: ز اسب = 'from the horse' (ز is the poetic preposition 'from'). اندر آمد is a compound verbal phrase meaning 'came in / alighted' — together 'alighted from'. سبک here functions adverbially 'light(ly)' modifying آمد, and شهریار is the subject 'the king'. Poetic word order places the adverb and title close together.
segments.s683-g22: همان = 'the same' (demonstrative). آنک is an archaic relative/pronominal form 'those who / that which'. بودند is the past plural of 'to be' indicating existence/presence. با اوسوار = 'with the horsemen/riders' (با = with, اوسوار = mounted men). Together this yields 'the same ones who were with the horsemen (were there).' Note the archaic relative آنک marking who is meant.
segments.s683-g23: جهانجوی is the subject, an agent-noun meaning 'the world-seeker' or 'ambitious man'. با introduces a comitative phrase 'with'. آن دو = 'those two' (the two companions), and خسرو پرست is a compound attributive 'Khosrow‑worshippers' (خسرو + پرست '‑worshipper') describing those two. The segment names the actors before the action.
segments.s683-g24: گرفت is the main past-tense verb 'seized/took'. از پی is an adverbial phrase 'after / in pursuit of' (lit. 'from following'), indicating that the seizure happened after something. واژ is a participial/adjectival word 'overturned / fallen' describing what had fallen. برسم functions as 'reached / came to' (attaining), and بدست here means 'into the hand' (i.e. brought to hand). Altogether: 'he seized (it) after it had fallen and brought it into his hand.'
segments.s683-g65: توگر is the conditional particle 'if' (here introducing a hypothetical). چاره‌جویی is a verbal-noun meaning 'seeking a remedy' and functions as the object/topic of دانی. دانی is 2nd-person present 'you know'; اکنون is an adverb 'now'; بساز is an imperative/command 'make/prepare' addressed to the addressee. The clause thus reads 'If you know a remedy, prepare it now.'
segments.s683-g66: هم is emphatic 'even' here. از links the following noun phrase ('from/of') to the idea of dependence. پاک یزدان is a reverential phrase 'the Pure God'. نه‌ای بی‌نیاز is the negated copula 'you are not free of need' (نه‌ای = 'you are not', بی‌نیاز = 'without need/self-sufficient'). The whole segment cautions that one is not independent even of the holy deity.
segments.s683-g67: بدو is a prepositional/adpositional clitic meaning 'to him/unto it' and precedes the verb in poetic word order. گفت is the past 'said' (here reported speech) and بندوی is the subject 'the bondman/servant'. کاین is the demonstrative/relative 'that/this' introducing the following noun phrase; تاج زر = 'gold crown' (زر qualifies تاج). The ordering is typical poetic V-S-Demonstrative-Noun.
segments.s683-g68: مرا ده is the request: مرا (me, object) + ده (imperative 'give'). همین is the demonstrative 'this very', modifying گوشوار 'earring/ornament'. و is the coordinating 'and', and کمر is 'girdle/belt'. Together: 'Give me this crown, this earring, and the girdle.'
segments.s683-g69: همان is the demonstrative 'that/that very'. لعل is a descriptive noun 'ruby' (color/gem), زرین is the adjective 'golden', چینی qualifies the robe as 'Chinese', and قبای is 'robe/garment'. Together they form a piled-up, poetic noun phrase: 'that ruby, golden Chinese robe.'
segments.s683-g70: چو means 'when/as'. من is the subject 'I'. پوشم is 1st-person singular present/subjunctive 'I put on / I wear'. این را is the direct object 'this (one)' with the object marker را. تو is the vocative 'you'. ایدر means 'here'. مپای is an imperative/negative or archaic verbal form meaning roughly 'do not be/stand' or 'do not come near' in this context — so the clause reads 'When I put this on, you (do) not be here.'
segments.s683-g71: برو is the imperative 'go'. با سپاهت is the prepositional phrase 'with your army' (با = with, سپاهت = your troops). هم here adds emphasis 'also/indeed'. اندر شتاب is a locution 'in haste' (اندر = in/within, شتاب = haste). The phrase issues a hurried command: 'Go with your army in haste.'
segments.s683-g72: چو is the comparative 'like/as'. کشتی is 'ship'. که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'. موجش = موج + ـش 'its wave(s)'. درآرد is a present/subjunctive verb 'causes to come forth / lifts / brings out'. ز آب is the prepositional phrase 'from the water' (ز = from, آب = water). The simile: 'like a ship that the waves lift out of the water.'
segments.s683-g113: برین بر here is an archaic/poetic locution meaning roughly “on/with this” or “regarding this”; که introduces the reported content clause. گفتم is the reporting verb “I said,” and نجویم is a present-subjunctive/negative-form (with the negative prefix ن-) meaning “I do not/should not seek” or “I will not seek.” زمان is the object of نجویم and here denotes Time/Fate rather than a moment.
segments.s683-g114: مگر introduces an exception and here functions as “unless.” یارمندی is a noun meaning “favor” or “kindness.” کند is third-person singular (optative/indicative form used for a hypothetical) “does/shows,” and آسمان, placed after the verb for poetic word order, is the subject “Heaven/sky.” Together: “unless Heaven shows favor.”
segments.s683-g115: نیاکان is the plural noun “ancestors.” ماآنک is an archaic fused expression (ما آن‌ک) meaning roughly “we/they of that time” or “those of ours,” and بودند is the past-plural copula “they were.” پیش is an adverb “before/long ago.” The segment places the ancestors in a past time frame.
segments.s683-g116: نگه داشتندی is an archaic past-plural compound meaning “they kept/kept watch” (نگه = keeping/watch, داشتندی = past ’they had/kept’). هم here conveys the sense “the same / also” modifying آیین (“rite, ceremony”), and وکیش is the conjunction + کیش (“and worship/faith”) written together in the manuscript. The clause states that they maintained the same ritual and religion.
segments.s683-g117: اگرچه is a concessive conjunction “although.” بدی بختشان is a nominal phrase “their bad fortune / ill luck” (بخت + possessive -شان). دیر ساز here describes that this ill fortune “made things late” or “caused delay” (دیر = late; ساز used adjectivally/nominally as ‘causing’). The clause concedes that hardship/delay occurred.
segments.s683-g118: ز is the poetic preposition “from / because of.” کهتر means “inferior / lower” (here likely ‘the lowly’ or ‘those of lesser rank’). نبرداشتندی is an archaic past negative “they did not take/accept” (نبرداشتن = not take), and نیاز is “need / aid / supplication.” The clause says they did not solicit or take assistance from inferiors.
segments.s683-g119: کنون is the temporal adverb “now.” آنچ (آن‌چ) is the relative “that which,” and ما را uses the first-person plural with the object/topic marker (’to/for us’ or marking what concerns us). به دل راز is a prepositional-possessive phrase “a secret in the heart” (به دل = in the heart; راز = secret), and بود is the past copula “was.” Altogether: “Now that which was secret in our heart was...”
segments.s683-g120: بگفتیم is the past first-person plural “we said/spoke” (poetic prefix ب- + گفتیم). چون here is causal “since/because,” بخت ناساز is a noun phrase “fortune (borkht) unkind/incompatible” (ناساز = adverse), and بود is the past copula “was.” The clause gives the reason for speaking: because fortune was unkind.
segments.s683-g129: دگر روز places a temporal adverb before the subject: دگر (‘another/next’) + روز (‘day’) = “the next day.” بندوی is the subject (an agent, ‘bondman/servant’). بربام here is a fused prepositional phrase (بر بام = “on the roof”), and شد is the past narrative verb (شد used here to mark the event: ‘went up / came to be on the roof’).
segments.s683-g130: ز is the poetic short form of از meaning ‘from/away from’; دیوار is the place noun ‘wall.’ تا here means ‘as far as / up to,’ and سوی is the directional noun ‘toward.’ بهرام is the proper name (object/direction). The final شد is the past narrative verb indicating movement/arrival (‘came/went’).
segments.s683-g131: بدو is a clitic/phrase meaning ‘to him’ (dative: ‘he said to him’); گفت is the reporting verb (‘said’). کامروز is a fronted temporal adverb ‘this very day / today,’ and شاه is the subject ‘the king.’ از نماز uses از in a temporal/relational sense ‘after / from (the) prayer,’ marking the circumstance linked to the king.
segments.s683-g132: همانا is a sentential particle meaning ‘verily / truly.’ نیاید is the negative present/subjunctive of آمدن used modally/temporally here (‘will not / does not come to’ = ‘will not undertake’). به introduces the noun کاری (‘any deed/enterprise’ here used as object = ‘an undertaking’), and فراز is a postpositive adverb/noun ‘on high / lofty,’ qualifying the kind of enterprise.
segments.s683-g133: چنین هم links back to the previous statement (‘thus also / likewise’). شب تیره is a noun + adjective order typical in poetry: ‘the dark night.’ بیدار بود is a past-tense predication where بیدار = ‘awake’ and بود is the past copula ‘was’ (i.e., ‘was awake’).
segments.s683-g134: This nominal clause reads as subject + modifiers: پرستنده (‘worshipper/devotee’) modified by پاک (‘pure’) and followed by دادار (‘the Creator’) in a possessive/relational sense (‘worshipper of the Creator’). بود is again the past copula ‘was’ — overall: ‘he was a pure worshipper of the Creator.’
segments.s683-g135: همان نیز here functions adverbially (‘likewise / similarly’). خورشید is the subject ‘the sun.’ گردد is the present/subjunctive or narrative form of شدن/گشتن meaning ‘becomes / rises / turns,’ and بلند is the complement ‘tall / high’ (i.e. ‘the sun becomes high’).
segments.s683-g136: زگرما is the fused prepositional phrase ز + گرما = ‘from/by the heat.’ نباید is an impersonal modal meaning ‘must not / should not’ (often used to express that something ought not to happen). که introduces the subordinate clause ‘that …,’ یابد is the present/subjunctive of یافتن (‘find / receive’) used here in the sense ‘suffer / incur,’ and گزند is the direct object ‘harm.’
segments.s683-g89: بدیدندش is the past plural perfective 'they saw him', with the object enclitic -ش attached to the verb; the clause-fronting of the verb is normal in epic diction. از دور is a prepositional phrase 'from afar' (literal order Verb + prepositional phrase), and با تاج زر ('with a crown of gold') uses با + noun to indicate accompaniment/appearance ('wearing a golden crown').
segments.s683-g90: This is a nominal list of ornaments. همان before طوق is a demonstrative/emphatic 'that very' or 'the same', then طوق, و links items, آن is the demonstrative 'that' before گوشوار, and کمر here means 'girdle/belt' — the three items are simply coordinated by و.
segments.s683-g91: همی‌گفت is an iterative past-form meaning 'kept saying' or 'would say' (همی- marks continuous/repeated action). هر کس 'every/each person' is the subject (indefinite), که introduces the content clause, and این خسروست is the identified phrase 'this is the king / this is Khosrow' — note the classical copula -ست (instead of modern است).
segments.s683-g92: This relative/explanatory clause (که ...) describes the person: با تاج و با جامه‌های نوست uses the repeated preposition با for rhythmic emphasis 'with a crown and with new garments'. نوست is 'is new' with the archaic/poetic copula -ست attached to نو (new).
segments.s683-g93: چو ... شد is a temporal clause 'when/when it came that ...'. بند here is a poetic word that can mean 'stanza/section' or 'bond' — in the poem it functions as an item that 'became' about him (classical usage); وی = 'him' (emphatic/poetic), شد is past 'became', and بی‌گمان is the adverb 'undoubtedly'. کان is an archaic demonstrative/marker referring to 'that' (not one of the modern آن/آن‌چنین forms) and سپاه = 'army'.
segments.s683-g94: همی‌بازنشناسد is a compound negative: همی- iterative prefix + بازنشناسد 'does not/keeps not recognizing' (here a habitual or continuing past sense in epic narration). The object marker را follows the pronoun او ('him'), and ز شاه literally 'from/as (the) king' marks the role in which they fail to recognise him — 'they do not recognise him as the king.'
segments.s683-g95: فرود آمد is a compound past verb 'he descended / he came down' (فرود + آمد). The sequence و جامه خویش تفت is the beginning of the next action: جامه خویش 'his own garment(s)' contains the possessive element خویش (here split orthographically as خوی + ش), and تفت is an adjective or epithet modifying جامه — its precise nuance is poetic/archaic and not matched by the cooking senses given, so it functions here as a descriptive modifier before the verb that follows.
segments.s683-g96: بپوشید is the past simple 'he put on / he dressed (himself)'. ناکام is a short adjectival/adverbial comment 'unsuccessful / thwarted' (placed after the verb cluster as an evaluative phrase). و بربام رفت 'and went up onto the roof' uses بر + بام (here written together as بربام) 'onto the roof' plus رفت 'went' as the motion verb.
segments.s683-g57: بدو is the prepositional clitic 'to him/unto him' placed before the verb (classical Persian often puts clitics before the verb). گفت is the verb 'said' (past) following that clitic; خسرو is the speaker name (subject) appearing after the verb by inverted/poetic word order. که introduces the reported clause; دانای چین is a noun phrase where دانای ('the wise one' / substantive use of the adjective دانا) is followed by its specification چین ('of China').
segments.s683-g58: یکی is an indefinite 'a certain/someone'; خوب modifies the manner of the action ('a fine' / 'skillful'); زد here is a verbal collocation (داستانی زد) where زدن with nouns often means 'to tell/produce' a tale in poetic usage (the dictionary senses given do not list this idiom, so it's effectively idiomatic). داستانی is the direct object 'a tale'; بر + ین here combine as برین = 'about this' (بر preposition 'on/about' + ین an older/orthographic form of 'این' = 'this'). The clause completes the reported speech begun by که.
segments.s683-g59: که is the subordinating conjunction 'that'. هرکو is an archaic/poetic indefinite 'whoever'. کند is the present/habitual 3rd-person singular 'does'/'performs'. بر درشاه is a prepositional phrase: بر 'at/on/toward' + درشاه 'the king's gate/court' (compound noun). کشت is a noun meaning 'killing' here, so کند ... کشت = 'does killing' / 'kills'.
segments.s683-g60: بیابد is a present/subjunctive/future-oriented verb form 'finds' or 'will find' used to state the consequence. بدان is the classical contraction of به + آن ('to/into that' or 'in that'), here linking the verb to the phrase 'گیتی اندر بهشت' — the speaker says one will 'find the world to be in paradise'. گیتی is 'the world'; اندر بهشت = 'in paradise' (اندر = in/within).
segments.s683-g61: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. دیوار شهر is a genitive construction 'the city's wall' (noun + possessor). اندر آمد is a compound verb 'came in' or 'fell in/collapsed' (اندر آمد often marks movement inward or, poetically, collapse). زپای is an adverbial phrase 'from the foot / from the base' (ز = from + پای = foot/base), specifying where the wall came in.
segments.s683-g62: کلاته is 'a small village / hamlet' (subject of the result clause). نباید is an impersonal negative 'cannot / must not' used narratively to mean 'could not' or 'would not'. که introduces the subordinate clause 'that (it) remain'; ماند is 'remain' (here dynamic/resultative), and بجای means 'in its place' (بِ + جای → 'in the place'). Together: the hamlet could not remain in its place.
segments.s683-g63: چو is 'when'. ناچیز is the adjective 'insignificant / small'. خواهد شدن is the periphrastic future/impending-change construction (خواهد + شدن) meaning 'is about to become / will become' — here it marks that the town is entering a state of insignificance. شارستان is 'the town / city' (subject).
segments.s683-g64: مماناد is an archaic/poetic negative past form meaning 'did not remain' (variant of نماند / نَمَاند). دیوار بیمارستان is the noun phrase 'the wall of the hospital' (دیوار + modifier بیمارستان). The clause reports that even that wall did not survive when the town declined.
segments.s683-g145: وگر introduces a conditional ('if'); کشته is a past participle meaning 'killed' placed before the light verb آید (here used in classical Persian to form a passive/result: 'be killed'). به دشت specifies the location ('in/to the plain') with poetic به for از/در, and نبرد names the setting ('battle'). The phrase as a whole is the protasis: 'if (he) is killed on the battlefield.'
segments.s683-g146: برآرد is a third‑person singular narrative/present verb here giving the apodosis ('(he) will/does bring/take'); ز ما = 'from us' using poetic ز for 'از'; نیز 'also/too' attaches to the following object; بهرام گرد is a proper name (Bahrām‑Gerd) serving as the object. The word order (verb before object) and the use of برآرد for a future/result is typical of epic diction.
segments.s683-g147: هم آن به که is an idiomatic evaluative phrase 'it is better that...' or 'better so that...'; باشیم is the first‑person plural present/subjunctive ('we be / we remain') used to express the recommended state. امروز is the time adverb 'today' and نیز reinforces 'also/too'.
segments.s683-g148: وگر introduces a subordinate conditional ('if'); خوردنی is a substantive participle meaning 'edible / something to eat'; نیست is the negative copula 'there is not'; بسیار چیز (inverted poetic order) means 'many things' or 'much' — together: 'if there is not much edible (to eat)'.
segments.s683-g149: مگر کو is a rhetorical interrogative meaning 'is there not one who...'; بدین is a poetic compound 'by this/with this' (ب + این) indicating 'with this sign/gesture'; هم نشان 'the same sign/token' (هم 'same/also' + نشان 'sign'); خوش‌منش is an epithet meaning 'courteous / good‑mannered' describing that person. The phrase asks whether someone of courteous bearing can be found.
segments.s683-g150: بیاید به از is an idiomatic comparative: بیاید ('it befits' / 'it is better') + به از ('better than'); جنگ و ز سرزنش are the two terms of comparison ('war and reproach'). Here بیاید is impersonal/evaluative ('would be preferable'), and وز is actually the fused form of و + ز ('and of/than'), an older poetic sequence joining the second item in the comparison.
segments.s683-g151: چنان هم همی‌بود is a continuous/imperfect narrative ('thus it was / thus it kept being'); تا شب means 'until night' and ز کوه is the ablative 'from the mountain' using poetic ز for از. This sets the temporal and source frame for the following action.
segments.s683-g152: برآمد is a past narrative verb 'arose / issued forth'; بگرد here functions as a verbal form meaning 'turning/about' (a poetic present/participle sense 'turn/turning'); اندر آمد is 'came in/entered' and گروه (placed after the verb, typical of poetic word order) is the subject 'the troop/group'. The clause describes the troop emerging from the mountain and coming in, with a sense of turning or circling.
segments.s683-g49: 'بدو گفت بندوی بس چاره‌ساز' literally stacks a dative clitic + verb + subject + modifier + noun. 'بدو' is the clitic phrase 'to him' placed before the verb (a common classical order), 'گفت' is the verb 'said', 'بندوی' is the speaker 'the bondman/servant', 'بس' is an intensifier ('quite/very' or 'enough' in older usage) modifying the following noun, and 'چاره‌ساز' is used as a descriptive noun/adjective 'problem‑solver, resourceful'.
segments.s683-g50: 'که آمدت دشمن بتنگی فراز' is a relative/nominal clause introduced by 'که' ('that' / introducing the content of what was said). 'آمدت' = آمد + enclitic ت, 'came/has come to you' (the enclitic marks the person affected). 'دشمن' is the subject 'an enemy', 'بتنگی' names the ill that accompanies him ('narrowness' = hardship), and 'فراز' is an adverbial/augment ('from on high' / 'upward, above') placed after the noun for poetic effect. Word order is slightly marked for rhythm: verb before subject and postpositive modifiers after the object.
segments.s683-g51: 'بدو گفت خسرو که‌ای نیک‌خواه' again puts the dative clitic 'بدو' before the verb: 'to him Khosrow said'. 'که‌ای' here is a vocative-like address ('O' + particle) attached to 'نیک‌خواه' (literally 'well‑wisher'); thus Khosrow is directly addressing the addressee as 'O well-wisher.'
segments.s683-g52: 'مرا اندرین کار بننمای راه' has the object pronoun 'مرا' ('me') fronted, 'اندرین کار' = 'in this affair' (اندر + این + کار), and the verb 'بنمای' is an optative/imperative form 'show (to me) / reveal'. 'راه' ('way') is the direct object of 'بنمای'. The order 'object – locative phrase – verb – object' is permissible in poetic/formal Persian and places emphasis on 'مرا' and 'اندرین کار.'
segments.s683-g53: بدو (to him) precedes the verb and marks the indirect object—literally “to him.” گفت is the past-tense verb “said” (3sg); word order is Verb‑Subject‑IndirectObject (poetic inversion): “to him said the bondman.” بندوی is the nominative subject “bondman/servant.” کای شهریار is a vocative phrase (“O, king/sovereign”), with کای an archaic vocative particle and شهریار the addressee.
segments.s683-g54: تو را = “you” + the object marker را (marking تو as definite/direct object). چاره سازم is a 1st‑person present/future/simple‑optative construction: سازم (“I make/devise”) with چاره (“remedy/solution”) as object → “I will devise a remedy.” بدین روزگار = “in these times” (بدین = به + این, an older/fused demonstrative meaning “this/these,” here glossed as a prepositional phrase + demonstrative; note the dataset split shows ب + دین but دین is not the noun “religion” here).
segments.s683-g55: ولیکن is a conjunction “but/however.” فدا کردن is the verbal phrase “to sacrifice” (فدا = sacrifice); کرده باشم is the perfect‑subjunctive/optative construction (کرده + باشم) used poetically to express willingness or a wish—here something like “may I have sacrificed” → idiomatically “I would give/my life be sacrificed.” روان (“soul/life”) is the thing offered; word order places the object after the compound verb.
segments.s683-g56: به پیش = “before/in the presence of” (به + پیش, a prepositional phrase). جهانجوی is a compound adjective/noun “world‑seeking,” i.e., ambitious; it modifies شاه جهان (“king of the world”), where شاه (king) + جهان (world) form the titular phrase. The whole string means “before the world‑seeking king of the world.”
